About the Ceremony

A Milestone Worth Celebrating

Pacific States University's Commencement Ceremony marks one of the most significant milestones in a student's academic journey. We gather to honor the dedication, resilience, and achievements of our graduating class.

Founded in 1928 and now operated by Konkuk University Foundation, PSU has a proud tradition of preparing students for meaningful careers and lives of purpose. This ceremony celebrates not just degrees conferred — but futures launched.

Family, friends, faculty, and the broader PSU community are warmly invited to join us in honoring our graduates on this extraordinary day.

"The journey of a thousand miles begins with one step — and ends with a celebration worthy of every step taken."

✦

Registration Checklist

  • Register by July 15, 2026 to guarantee cap & gown sizing
  • Each graduate may invite up to 4 guests
  • Guests receive digital tickets via email after registration
  • Graduates must wear official PSU academic regalia
  • Arrive by 1:00 PM — ceremony begins at 2:00 PM sharp
  • Parking information emailed to registered attendees

Ceremony Schedule

  • 1:00 PM — Doors open, seating begins
  • 1:45 PM — Graduates assemble for processional
  • 2:00 PM — Procession & ceremony begins
  • 2:10 PM — Welcome & opening remarks
  • 2:25 PM — Keynote address
  • 3:00 PM — Conferral of degrees
  • 4:30 PM — Recessional & photography
  • 5:00 PM — Reception on campus grounds

Contact & Support

  • Website: www.psuca.edu
  • Email: registrar@psuca.edu
  • Office hours: Mon–Fri, 9AM–5PM

Frequently Asked Questions

What You Need to Know

How many guests can I bring?

Each graduate may invite up to 4 guests. Additional tickets may be available on a waitlist — contact the Registrar for availability.

What should graduates wear?

All graduates must wear official PSU academic regalia — cap, gown, and hood. Regalia can be ordered via the PSU Bookstore or at check-in.

Will the ceremony be livestreamed?

Yes, a livestream link will be emailed to all registered attendees prior to the ceremony for those unable to attend in person.

Is parking available on campus?

Complimentary parking is available for registered attendees. A parking map will be sent to all registered graduates and guests beforehand.

What if I have accessibility needs?

PSU is fully committed to accessibility. Wheelchair seating, sign language interpretation, and hearing loop access are available with advance notice via registration.

When is the registration deadline?

Registration closes July 15, 2026. Early registration is encouraged for cap & gown sizing, seating arrangements, and name pronunciation review.

How is my name announced?

Graduates can submit the phonetic spelling or a pronunciation guide for their name during registration to ensure correct announcement during the ceremony.

Where can I find more information?

Visit www.psuca.edu for complete information about programs, alumni services, and commencement updates.
